message:
🌱 Add buildpacks: Use fully qualified image names (podman compatibility) mission (#1148)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "buildpacks-1218-use-fully-qualified-image-names-podman-compatibility",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "buildpacks: Use fully qualified image names (podman compatibility)",
+    "description": "Use fully qualified image names (podman compatibility). Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current buildpacks setup",
+        "description": "Verify your buildpacks version and configuration:\n```bash\npack version\n```\nThis feature requires a working buildpacks installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review buildpacks configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant buildpacks configuration:\nFor podman compatibility (and more) it would be good to use fully qualified image names ( = include registry in image name)\n\nExample: Instead of:\n\n```\nbuildpacksio/lifecycle:0.11.3\n```\n\nuse:\n```\ndocker.io/buildpacksio/lifecycle:0.11.3\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Use fully qualified image names (podman compatibility)",
+        "description": "I like this suggestion although I know there will be some necessary discussion about ease-of-use considering that there's a large number of users that are coming from the docker CLI where the host is assumed if missing.\n```yaml\nbuildpacksio/lifecycle:0.11.3\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"Use fully qualified image names (podman compatibility)\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "I like this suggestion although I know there will be some necessary discussion about ease-of-use considering that there's a large number of users that are coming from the docker CLI where the host is assumed if missing.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "buildpacksio/lifecycle:0.11.3",
+        "docker.io/buildpacksio/lifecycle:0.11.3"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
